Name: Land Bank Infrastructure Bond 2021A (ZAR 3.0 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 15-year fixed-rate bond issued by Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank) under its ZAR 25B Domestic Medium-Term Note Programme. Coupon 9.0% p.a. Proceeds finance Land Bank's primary agricultural mortgage and production lending. JSE listing ref JSE/LB/2021/001. NT guarantee. S&P: A(za) National Scale. Land Bank Act No. 15 of 2002.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Infrastructure Bond 2022A (ZAR 2.5 billion) – Agricultural Mortgage Backed
Description: 10-year bond collateralised by Land Bank's ZAR 18B agricultural mortgage book (65,000 registered farm mortgages). Coupon 9.5% p.a. FSCA-approved. JSE-listed. Rated S&P A(za). NT guarantee letter. Proceeds refinance Land Bank's medium-term lending book and support the OPAP (Agri Finance Recapitalisation Programme). JSE ref JSE/LB/2022/003.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Social Bond 2022B (ZAR 1.8 billion) – Smallholder Farmer Finance
Description: 8-year social bond aligned with ICMA Social Bond Principles. Coupon 9.25% p.a. Proceeds exclusively fund Land Bank's Retail Lending (RL) Division loans to emerging and black smallholder farmers under CASP, ILIMA/LETSEMA and BELA-BELA programmes. Target: 8,500 new smallholder borrowers. JSE Sustainability Segment. NT approval. DALRRD endorsement.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Green Bond 2023A (ZAR 1.2 billion) – CBI Climate-Smart Agriculture
Description: 7-year green bond certified by the Climate Bonds Initiative (CBI) – Agriculture, Land Use and Forestry category. Coupon 8.75% p.a. Proceeds fund climate-smart agriculture loans: conservation tillage, drip irrigation (DWS), solar pump systems, soil carbon sequestration and agroforestry. JSE Sustainability Segment. NT approval. Sustainalytics SPO.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Infrastructure Bond 2024A (ZAR 4.1 billion)
Description: 12-year bond – largest single Land Bank issuance since 2020 recapitalisation. Coupon 10.5% p.a. Proceeds fund large-scale commercial agri-lending and irrigation infrastructure finance: 3 major irrigation schemes (Vaalharts, Breede River, Olifants River upgrades), 12 grain storage silo complexes and agri-logistics. JSE listing ref JSE/LB/2024/001. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Sustainable Agriculture Bond 2024B (ZAR 950 million)
Description: 5-year sustainability-linked bond with KPI ratchet: coupon steps down 0.1% for each 10% increase in black farmer borrower share above 35% baseline. Coupon starting 9.25% p.a. ICMA Sustainability-Linked Bond Principles. JSE Sustainability Segment. DALRRD/DTIC BBBEE Agri Financing target. NT approval ref NT/SLB/LB/2024/001.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Equity Token – Government of SA 100% Shareholder Holding
Description: Tokenised equity representing the Government of South Africa's 100% ownership of Land Bank. Land Bank total assets: ZAR 52B (Q4 2024 audited). Net equity post-2021 NT recapitalisation: ZAR 8.4B. NT recapitalisation amount: ZAR 7B (2021–2024) following 2020 default event. Land Bank Act No. 15 of 2002. Annual shareholders compact with DALRRD and NT.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Equity – AgriSETA Skills Training Fund Partnership (20% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Land Bank's 20% strategic stake in the AgriSETA–Land Bank Agricultural Finance Skills Programme JV. Annual disbursement: ZAR 180M to train 4,500 emerging farmers in financial literacy, record keeping and agri-finance management. Land Bank equity contribution: ZAR 120M. AgriSETA levy funding: ZAR 60M. DALRRD skills programme endorsement.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Equity – Black Farmer Development Fund (25% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Land Bank's 25% anchor stake in the Black Farmer Development Fund (BFDF) – a ZAR 2.4B blended finance vehicle co-funded with DBSA, GEPF and Nedbank. Fund targets 1,200 black commercial and emerging farmers with equity, quasi-equity and grants. Land Bank committed equity: ZAR 600M. DALRRD CASP grant complement: ZAR 480M.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Equity – Agri-Digital Finance Platform Subsidiary (51% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Land Bank's 51% controlling stake in Agri-Digital Finance Platform (Pty) Ltd – a digital lending and farm management platform for smallholder farmers. Platform provides mobile-first loan applications, digital collateral assessment and remote sensing crop monitoring. JV partner: Farmers Business Network (FBN, 49%). Land Bank equity: ZAR 95M.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank NT Government Recapitalisation Guarantee (ZAR 10 billion)
Description: National Treasury sovereign guarantee covering Land Bank's total senior unsecured debt portfolio (ZAR 10B). Issued under PFMA s.70 following Land Bank's 2020 default on ZAR 8B callable debt. NT recapitalisation tranche 4 completed 2024. Guarantee enables Land Bank's return to investment grade: S&P A(za), Fitch A(za). NT ref NT-RECAP-LB-2021/2024.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank–AfDB Agricultural Finance Credit Line (USD 200 million)
Description: AfDB long-term credit line to Land Bank for on-lending to South African commercial and smallholder farmers. AfDB loan ref ADB/RSA/LB/2023/001. 20-year term, 5-year grace, AfDB benchmark + 0.9%. Focus: agri-value chain lending, climate-smart agriculture and AGRI-MAN agri-management programme. NT sovereign guarantee. First disbursement: USD 80M to KZN and Limpopo farmers.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank–World Bank IBRD Agriculture Support Facility (USD 300 million)
Description: IBRD programme-for-results lending facility (P4R) for Land Bank's smallholder transformation programme. World Bank project P181234. NT on-lends to Land Bank at 0.75% service charge. 25-year term, 7-year grace. Disbursements trigger smallholder lending KPIs (number of black farmer loans, women-owned farm loans, climate-smart adoption). NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank USAID DCA Partial Risk Guarantee (USD 40 million ceiling)
Description: USAID Development Credit Authority guarantee covering 50% of principal loss on Land Bank loans to women-owned farms, agri-SMEs and beginning farmers in North West, Free State and KwaZulu-Natal. DCA agreement USAID/RSA/DCA/2022/003. Ceiling: USD 40M. Mobilises Land Bank lending at 2% below standard rate to 2,400 qualifying borrowers. USAID/South Africa mission ref.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank–EIB Agricultural Climate Finance Facility (EUR 200 million)
Description: European Investment Bank climate-smart agriculture facility for Land Bank on-lending. EIB agreement EIB/RSA/LB/2024/001. 20-year term, EIB Climate Action Lending Rate + 1.2%. Focus: precision agriculture technology adoption, water-efficient irrigation, renewable energy on farms and climate-resilient crop varieties. NT guarantee. EU Global Gateway alignment. First disbursement EUR 60M.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Limpopo Irrigation Scheme Revenue Bond (ZAR 1.8 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by water use levy revenues and irrigation service fees from the Limpopo Valley Water Scheme (LVWS): 42,000 ha irrigated under Schoemansdal, Nwanedzi and Luvuvhu schemes. Annual revenue: ZAR 420M from 1,200 commercial irrigators and 680 smallholders. DSCR: 1.55×. DWS water use authorisation WUA/LIM/2022. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Vaalharts Irrigation Scheme Revenue Bond (ZAR 2.4 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by irrigation infrastructure rentals and water levy from Vaalharts Irrigation Scheme, Northern Cape (largest irrigation scheme in SA: 37,000 ha maize, wheat, peanuts, cotton). Annual DWS water levy: ZAR 580M. Infrastructure service fees: ZAR 120M. DSCR: 1.62×. Land Bank bridge financing for TCTA canal rehabilitation: ZAR 1.4B. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Breede Valley Irrigation Revenue Bond (ZAR 1.1 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by Breede-Gouritz Catchment Management Agency (CMA) water tariff revenues for the Breede Valley Irrigation Scheme, Western Cape: 28,000 ha (apples, pears, grapes, hops). Annual CMA tariff revenue: ZAR 280M. Land Bank bridge financing for dyke and canal rehabilitation. DSCR: 1.48×. DWS WUA registration BRV-WUA-2023. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Olifants River Agricultural Revenue Bond (ZAR 680 million)
Description: Revenue bond backed by Olifants River Water Resources Development Project irrigation infrastructure revenues: 18,500 ha citrus, table grapes and stone fruit in Citrusdal/Clanwilliam (Cederberg). Annual CMA water levy: ZAR 165M. TCTA bulk supply contribution. DSCR: 1.42×. DWS General Authorisation. Land Bank co-financer for WUE infrastructure upgrade.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Free State Grain Silo Complex Revenue Bond (ZAR 920 million)
Description: Revenue bond backed by grain storage and handling fees from 12 Land Bank-financed grain silo complexes in Free State (combined capacity: 820,000 t). Annual storage and handling revenue: ZAR 218M from Senwes, OVK, Afgri and Griekwaland-Wes Cooperative tenants. DSCR: 1.58×. Silos collateralised under SAFEX Grade 1 silo certificates. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Vaalharts Irrigation Canal & Pump Station Infrastructure Asset
Description: Tokenised infrastructure asset representing the Vaalharts Irrigation Scheme main canal system (280 km), pump stations (8), and tailwater recovery dams (4) owned by TCTA and managed by Land Bank under OPAP. Asset value based on 2024 replacement cost and infrastructure condition assessment. TCTA-Land Bank co-management agreement VHS-MGMT-2023. Serves 37,000 ha irrigated land.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Limpopo Valley Water Infrastructure Asset
Description: Tokenised infrastructure asset for the Limpopo Valley Water Scheme (LVWS) civil infrastructure: Nwamitwa Dam (135 Mm³), Nandoni Dam (270 Mm³), inter-basin transfer tunnels and 380 km of concrete-lined canals serving Vhembe and Mopani districts. Infrastructure co-owned with TCTA and DWS. Asset replacement value ZAR 12B. Land Bank balance sheet value: ZAR 3.8B.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank National Grain Storage Network – 12 Silo Complexes (820,000 t capacity)
Description: Tokenised grain storage infrastructure asset: 12 Land Bank-owned silo complexes in Free State, North West and Mpumalanga (combined 820,000 t capacity). Asset includes: steel bin silos, flat storage warehouses, grain dryers, weigh bridges and loading facilities. Constructed 2015–2022. Book value: ZAR 2.4B. Operated under lease by Senwes, OVK, Afgri and GWK cooperatives.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Limpopo Smallholder Agri-Finance PPP Token (ZAR 1.6 billion)
Description: PPP token for Land Bank–DALRRD–Massmart Limpopo Agri-Corridor: agri-finance, extension services and guaranteed offtake for 2,400 smallholder vegetable and citrus farmers. Land Bank provides ZAR 840M in loans and guarantees. DALRRD CASP: ZAR 320M. Massmart pre-financing: ZAR 440M. PFMA s.76 PPP registered. NT approval NT-PPP/LIM/LB/2024/001. Target 12,000 rural jobs.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)

Name: Land Bank Water-Efficient Farming PPP Token – Western Cape (ZAR 1.2 billion)
Description: PPP token for Land Bank–DWS–DAFF Western Cape Water-Efficient Farming Programme: retrofitting 8,500 ha from flood/sprinkler to precision drip irrigation. Private technology partner: Netafim SA (Israel). Land Bank finance: ZAR 680M for farmer equipment loans. WCDE irrigation incentive: ZAR 280M. EU Green Deal financing: EUR 20M. Water saving: 35% reduction in irrigation abstraction per ha.
OwnerName: Land and Agricultural Development Bank of South Africa (Land Bank)
